text:
it's a night and day difference in how much more i understand about human nature and discernment now than a i did a year ago. the biggest paradigm shift for me was understanding blame internalizers vs. blame externalizers and filtering my social circles entirely for ppl who know how to + will take accountability. it's okay to make mistakes and get things wrong (as long as they're not egregious) but accountability is how much ownership someone takes when made aware of them.

how to thrive socially on this basis is essentially:
1) be someone who takes accountability (i already was, and i have been for a while)
2) become someone who isn't *overly accountable* for what is genuinely other ppls' work to do/their doing
3) cut out ppl who lack accountability bc they will drain you (or if you can't, at least have them in the outer "acquaintance" category but not in your inner circle)
4) cut out anyone who holds long-standing grudges, contempt, or resentment even long after other people take full and genuine accountability.
5) you're left with a circle that has integrity and reliability, believes mostly in the good in ppl, and otherwise takes feedback well

text:
I got asked by guys I talked to from my dating survey (in a friendly way) "Wait, so like...what's wrong with you? How are you still single? Don't you meet ppl in LA?"

Subtext: "Is she low-key hot but crazy? And actually undatable?"
Jk. And the answer's no 😆

The actual answer to it was...I wasn't rly venturing outside safe, surrounding suburbia where everyone is either 50 years old or their teenage kid. Now I do, and yeah, ppl are everywhere. I just wasn't around ppl my age 😅😆

If you're struggling to meet ppl IRL my advice is to hang in neighborhoods where there's ppl living your age and your lifestyle! I know it sounds obvious to some, but I was like "how do ppl get approached so often irl?" This is how

text: i'm noticing this recurring pattern where i keep joking i'm evil and i think it's coming from guilt i'm internalizing about not responding in a timely manner to ppl wanting to connect, hang, etc - sometimes i actually shut down a little. i prolong responding for days before i send that text telling someone they're awesome, but i don't feel the same. i can't keep up with messages and social planning sometimes, and other times i don't have the capacity on my social calendar, and other times i just don't feel the same way about a person. i know it's not something to "feel bad about" and i feel immense gratitude for ppl appreciating me (and i appreciate them as ppl too! truly) but as someone that had to work through people-pleasing tendencies, i feel guilt. it's making me call myself evil even jokingly, but i'm not evil

text:
I actually feel genuine exhaustion about some of the red pill advice on here that's all like "men don't want a woman that's too in her masculine and they don't need you to be ambitious".. what?

The red pill dating advice might have grains of truth where many women do the chasing but they can instead relax into existence and let men show up, but applied to extremes it's disempowering and makes female ambition sound like some "hah, aw, that's cute honey" thing. It feels like it's written by some "normalize making yourself smaller so we can feel bigger" group

Interestingly, and I didn't filter on this, but I noticed that the men who were at the top of my dating survey reported their yin/yang ratio as 40/60 and I would report mine as 60/40. They were motivated and ambitious, and had pensive, softer sides

 There's a complement for everyone

It feels like it's written by some "normalize making yourself smaller so we can feel bigger" group

Interestingly, and I didn't filter on this, but I noticed that the men who were at the top of my dating survey reported their yin/yang ratio as 40/60 and I would report mine as 60/40. They were motivated and ambitious, and had pensive, softer sides

 There's a complement for everyone

text:
I love the power of imagination bc in that moment it's like created reality - as in, literally translates to autonomic nervous system effects that drive your physical reality too. For this reason, deities as a symbol for larger existential forces at play are effective. 

There's real, integrative power and safety to be experienced when you're able to subversively internalize the most fear-inducing deities as your protectors. It's not even having to believe in God, necessarily; it's evoking a symbol.

I'll explain this with a real, small, recent example:

Friends and I are prepping for this spiritual practice and one friend says "leave your Labubus behind, they look like the Pazuzu demon and it's bad vibes"
So I'm like "what's Pazuzu" and ofc look it up. At first I'm like "cool, a demon god, we can revere those too" (you know me) but thennnn the exorcist screen caps pop up and I'm like "oh it's kinda cute - ok wait, hellllll no" but it's imprinted into my brain

But then I remember how I used to be so spooked by the image of Kali until I studied her symbolism. A form and image reify this concept that the nature of existence can be life-creating, protective, and life-sustaining like a universal mother (you were born and you are here) but also destructive - where there is life, there is death, and the same world that nurtures you will take you with it in its cycles. And so, she became a symbol of "I'll fight off your horrors but we're all equal - I will come for you too". And when your deity looks demonic and scarier than anything that scares you, it's effectively this image-driven protection spell. So Pazuzu became no more in my brain and my body and mind chilled out

But then, Pazuzu, I read, was considered evil but sometimes worshipped to protect against other forces? So coming back to full circle, it's honestly whatever powerful forces in this world you want to reify through visual metaphor. It's like casting your own psychological protection spells and being backed by gods that help you slow down, breathe, see the refraction of the light they shine on reality, and accept it as you envision it

text:
there's this phenomena where i can find someone extremely attractive but in way that's not fully aligned and therefore not want to date them, but it still stands that they're extremely attractive. a full buy-in typically operates on three types of "yes":

body yes - raw sexual attraction that your body registers, independent of your thoughts or emotions; alone, it's the shallowest so it's easy come easy go, meaning that it might be the first to perceive (this person might turn your head or pull your gaze) but without either of the other two yeses it doesn't rly mean much to me
heart yes - feeling genuine connection/feeling seen by them. this is built through substantial conversation
head yes - this person logically makes sense. this is the decision layer. it's the last over time if the other yeses are there, or the first if there's something about the person that just wouldn't have you evaluating them for dating. eg. wants different things out of life, is at a different life stage, misalignment of values, misalignment for timing, etc. 

breakdown of permutations and what they mean in my head:
heart + head yes, body no = friends and friendly collaborators! awesome ones
body + heart yes, but head no = romantic potential but without commitment, or experiencing the occasional tension of a crush. your head can buy-in later, sometimes. this one, i find, is the most tense for me personally
body + head yes but heart no = ??? i don't rly get this? i think i need at leasttt a heart yes to even have any kind of romantic/sexual consideration. maybe like some kinda sapiosexual flirationship but idk what anything means without heart. maybe this is like ur hot coworker or something. i guess it's in the fantasy territory
body yes only = i guess this is like ONS territory, but with the way i work this feels vapid/unfulfilling and isn't my style
heart yes only = i don't think this exists as only! usually head says "yes" to friends where there's a heart connection already :)
head yes = intellectual partners, colleagues, collaborators, etc. where you have more of a working/professional relationship. they can have potential for heart yes later if you get to know them

the answer to "why are you still single" is that ppl frequently hit 2/3 of the yeses above for me (SO many heart + head yeses and the occasional body + heart yes without my head buying in) but i don't feel like i'm far away from 3/3, either

text:
Bi attraction explained by my experience (other ppl may vary): ppl need to stop assuming bi girls have a crush on everyone they're like "omgggg ily" and wanna be around bc a lot of this is still platonic love. there's actually a hardcore delimiter for romantic potential in my head. I am actually not sexually attracted to most ppl (men or women)! Being attracted to 2-5% of ppl is still 2-5%. Lots of beautiful female friends (like rly pretty ones!) and literally noneeee of them my type. If anything, because I understand this so well in my head and I also need to live life with same-gender friends, I actually tend to treat male and female friends the same when it's platonic but *so similarly* that it confuses hopeful men bc yes I can actually be rlyyy lovey in a platonic way with zero sexual interest

If you're curious about what's my type of woman? Probably feminine, similar build/stature/height/size to me (I don't wanna feel like "daddy" to you or whatever the fk I wanna be in my feminine parity!!! Vs attraction to men is wanting to feel into my feminine *polarity* eg. I actually wanna feel smaller than him), and she has the same indescribable energy bc she's also bi - straight women don't rly have this energy, I can't describe it. It's an integrated lead/follow energy with similar presence

Sooo yes I can go to a kspa and see my pretty female friends nude and feel nothing at all - there's typically no draw (they're straight and they ping as straight to me)
The same wouldn't apply for my majority straight male friends

All that being said I date intentionally and legit just wanna settle down and have kids so I only actually date men haha 
BUT I am highly likely to let my type of woman touch me in the wild hehe (if ur a random man and idk you at all and I just met you, and you try the same in the wild, I will likely imagine scratching u)

And yessss monogamous, loyal, bi girls exist

text:
alright, so i am entirely supportive of Palestinian human rights and liberation, and have been so pretty much my entire life thanks to my parents educating me about this issue since i could understand politics. you're lashing out at the wrong person who has actually taken action offline in MANY ways for this cause since.. i don't even know when? 2008 middle school days? earlier??

i'm really sorry that this is happening to your family, genuinely. it's tragic, and i felt similar sentiments about social media around oct 2023 and beyond but the world is huge with a lot happening at once. you have no idea how ppl are actually taking action offline and how. it's so reductive to call my reflections self-help BS. comparing and dismissing peoples' lives gets ppl absolutely nowhere. TPOT is not where i go to make or impact sociopolitical change. the dance club is not where i go to learn vibecoding. the meditation circle is not where i go to learn how to write poetry. activist circles are not where i go to party and dance my problems away

anyway, thank you for owning this and for your apology. again, this puts me in a bit of bind bc 1) yes i empathize with your pain and i truly am sorry that's happening but 2) i'm not the one to pull this at, and you have no idea who i am offline. and i understand you owned it (which i appreciate)

text: If someone claims to be a teacher or enlightened but then criticizes the way you live your life when it's harming no one, that is questionable. A local yoga teacher that gave me weird vibes turned out to be controlling and cultish (as I found out from mutuals) and the first red flag was how critical he was in tone of alternative spiritual paths (he practices Kriya and follows Yogananda, and bashed Advaita Vedanta). Ppl claiming to be spiritual teachers on here leaving biting comments on innocuous posts isn't it, either. Where have I felt like someone was a teacher? Some monks at the temple and traveling monks from other temples who are endlessly compassionate, incredible listeners, and thoughtful responders without an ounce of self-serving performance (and it's not all monks, because some are repressing) - there's a felt difference

text:
The irony is those "well I don't need other people's validation like you" ppl are the ones that are actually likely to invalidate and damage you into a self-doubting hell if you stick around them - so much that you stop being self-validating and start to need other people's validation to help pull you out of that hellhole. And guess what? That's not a bad thing.

I needed to hear that I was lovely, feminine, and delightful to be around from friends. I needed to hear that my energy and poetic brain were lovely instead of "dissociated" and "reminiscent of awkward ppl who didn't grow up cool in school so they quietly developed talents" from someone that was jealous of creativity bc they didn't feel like they had it.

Fk this messaging. Sometimes validation goes a long way after years of invalidation that drags you from your luminous and radiant baseline. Your loving and validating friends WILL heal you. And it's not like you would actually be going around asking for validation, but to simply be around people who actually say nice, genuine things and occasionally take interest in your expressions or creations makes all the difference

text:
Sometimes I'm like "dang does this sound too victimy" bc I can pretty much hear his voice going "this is a victim mindset" and "we were both in pain" - and this false equivalency is the primary blocker that keeps you from processing abuse properly 

I'm DONE with spirituality and forgiveness culture being used to gaslight and sidestep a need for accountability and twist the story. "You sound bitter. I have forgiven you, why don't you forgive me? You're not loving like me, now". Because we're not the same.

My mom had a rough life. When i was young she would slap the shit out of me for small things and then cry about it bc she felt like a bad mom. I'd go to comfort her, my face still stinging. Were we both in pain? Yeah. Are we the same? Fuck no. Does her childhood explain the abuse? Yes. Does it excuse it? Fuckkkk no.

can i say something without everyone getting mad (or maybe you can get mad, it's fine)

i hate a win/lose mindset (ESPECIALLY in conflicts with friends and in romantic relationships) and i hate arguments. i would rather find resolution and harmony; i'll always try to achieve clarity and use reason, and i don't lose my temper like...ever

but my toxic trait (it's not that toxic, someone is probably being a downright asshole to me or someone who doesn't deserve it) is probably a deep, felt sense of "if you force this into an argument, i actually will NEVER fucking lose the argument and i know this, and i won't break - but you might set yourself on fire if you're not careful" and winning it is kind of..fun

text:
the shitty relationships i was in were precisely so long because:

1) i didn't know what "better" looked like. i didn't have healthy relationship models
antidote: trust the perspectives of your healthy engaged/married/"happy in their relationship + in life" friends and observe how they are! if you don't have any, GO MAKE SOME pls it'll save you from dealing with garbage. you need an updated model

2) i feared leaving bc "i'm too old to start over" and i had these thoughts between ages 23-25 (hit a "now or never, just rip the bandaid off" at 25 and did leave) and another one at 26-29 but this is just simply not true
antidote: look at all the ppl finding love in their 30s!! there's a ton of examples. understand "too old" is some kind of stupidass sexist myth. and women CAN have kids at 35+, fertility tech has gotten much much better. don't go through hell just bc of your fears

3) i didn't know that men could be kind, sweet, and care about my pain.
antidote: make quality male friends. and don't be gaslit into distancing from them if you're both respectful about boundaries that you should have when you're in a committed, monogamous relationship. less exposure to good and respectful men = perfect control tactic for men who want you to stick around for their bad behavior and disrespect you

text:
okayy i guess i'll thread how i'm doing it now:
- wireframe or mockup first in figma and then input screenshots
- define objects, variables, and data structures (you don't even have to be an engineer, you can use LLMs but might take more prompting so preferrably one you're subscribed to!)
- get LLM to do security proofing, specify which vibecoder and db you're using. generate the code
- feed screenshots + code into vibecoder

completely open to process improvements :)

raw.full_text:
okayy i guess i'll thread how i'm doing it now:

- define objects, variables, and data structures (you don't even have to be an engineer, you can use LLMs but might take more prompting so preferrably one you're subscribed to!)
- get LLM to do security proofing, specify which vibecoder and db you're using. generate the code
- feed screenshots + code into vibecoder

completely open to process improvements :)

text:
from chatGPT "Your Workflow (critique)

1. Wireframe/mockup in Figma → input screenshots
✅ Smart. Way better than describing UI in text.
⚠️ Pitfall: Uploading too many high-res screenshots at once can chew credits. Cropping to just the relevant section of the UI is more efficient.
2. Define objects, variables, and data structures
✅ Huge win. This reduces back-and-forth later.
⚠️ If you let the LLM “invent” data structures without constraints, it often produces bloat → you end up regenerating. Write just enough schema yourself before handing it off.
3. Get LLM to do security proofing, specify vibecoder + DB
✅ Good practice. Explicit stack saves confusion.
⚠️ “Security proofing” can generate tons of verbose suggestions. A trick: ask it only for delta fixes (“What changes should I make to harden this code?”) instead of full rewrites.

4. Feed screenshots + code into vibecoder
✅ Solid. Combining visual + code context boosts accuracy.
⚠️ Don’t feed entire codebases. Instead, feed just the snippet you want improved + the screenshot context.
How to Vibecode Better (credit-wise + workflow-wise)
Chunk small.
One screen, one feature, one data model at a time. Avoid “big dumps” — they’re expensive and often worse quality.

Reuse good generations.
When vibecoder produces a good button, modal, or flow — copy it into a snippet library (Notion, GitHub, or even a Google Doc). Then reference:
“Make this screen, but follow the same button code as in [snippet].”
Much cheaper than asking for fresh each time.
Optimize screenshot uploads.

Crop only to the component/section
Use medium resolution (not giant PNGs)
Label the screenshot filenames with intent (e.g. login_wireframe.png)

Prompt incrementally.
Instead of: “Build me the login screen with X, Y, Z,”
Do:

“Generate skeleton layout from screenshot”
“Add form validation”

“Add error states”
This modularity reduces costly rewrites.
Cache your “meta-prompts.”
Keep a few “good” scaffolding prompts saved (security review, data model sanity check, UI consistency check). Paste them in when needed instead of re-inventing.

Learn to skim + prune code.
Before regenerating, ask: “Can I just tweak this manually?” Sometimes editing 3 lines yourself saves 50 credits.
Leverage LLM as explainer, not just generator.
Use: “Explain what this function does line by line.” Costs less than regenerating and teaches you how to patch by hand."

i guess i rly could just self-teach with chatGPT-5 but i don't wanna be overly reliant 😅

text:
more chatGPT stuff bc i complained about credit burn:

"🌟 Golden Path Vibecoding Workflow
Phase 1: Pre-build (no credits burned)

Wireframe first.
Figma / Excalidraw / even sketches.
Keep layouts modular (1 screen = 1 frame).

Define your data.
List tables + key fields in plain English.
Decide what variables your app absolutely needs (auth, user prefs, objects, etc.).

Save your “meta-prompts.”
Security check prompt
“Explain this code” prompt
“Optimize for reusability” prompt

Phase 2: First pass (low-cost runs)

Upload only the cropped screenshot for the screen/component you’re working on.
Don’t dump a whole multi-screen mockup.
Medium resolution PNG/JPG.

Generate skeleton first, then details.
Step 1: “Layout only” (positions, containers, spacing).
Step 2: “Add logic/validation.”
Step 3: “Add polish/animations.”

Explicitly declare stack.
“I’m building this in Lovable with X database.”
Prevents it from inventing mismatched imports.

Phase 3: Refinement (credits saved by reusing & patching)

Build a snippet library.
Copy good code chunks into Notion/GitHub.
Reuse with: “Make this modal, but use the same code style as [snippet].”

Patch manually before regenerating.
If you can fix it in <5 lines, do it yourself.
Ask LLM for an explanation of unclear code before you regen.

Delta security checks.
Instead of: “Make this secure,”
Say: “List vulnerabilities in this snippet + suggest fixes.”

Phase 4: Versioning + feedback

Save every working build.
Export/commit after each “stable checkpoint.”
Rollbacks are cheaper than regenerating.

Iterate with screenshots + code together.
Better than text-only.
But don’t re-upload old screenshots unless something changed.

Test early, test often.
Run small pieces in sandbox.
Catch breakage before feeding the whole thing back into Lovable.

✅ This way, each loop is small + modular, you minimize regen, and you end up with a personal “snippet kit” that makes later projects faster and cheaper."

text:
i'm not saying i'm kegan 5 but i wish ppl would adopt kegan 5 (even cosplay it, idgaf) for a bit such that they adopt the thinking framework of multiple truths when it comes to buzz like Charlie Kirk BUT ALSO have stances on PRINCIPLE

hold multiple truths...but don't use it as an excuse to be wishy-washy and centrist without a stance like some ineffectual and spineless "peacekeeper", either. because that's not keeping the peace, that's just being passive and spineless. know your principles and have your opinions

eg. personal model, crosschecked with principles of peace, nonviolence, inclusivity, equity, and actions oriented on effective outcomes and productive conversation:

yes he incited hate and said stupid shit and this is irresponsible and feeds a divisive culture and violence
yes on principle physical violence and murder is absolutely not the answer
yes ppl should talk about death/murder/issues/genocide when it's not a white public american male with a large platform being murdered
yes there's dramatic irony in his death
yes there's poetic justice in his death but it's not morally absolute justice because murder in all forms (genocide, a jerk getting shot, a senseless single killing, systemic deaths of neglected ppl in an inequitable society) is morally reprehensible, *on principle* 
no, the ppl laughing about his death aren't monsters either or evil, they're laughing at dramatic irony but they still care about *something*
no, the ppl saying RIP about him and not Gaza aren't monsters or evil, they're awareness and focus is a product of mainstream media bias and societal emphasis and it doesn't absolve them of responsibility of education and expanding their awareness but ppl shaming them for a relatively narrow focus is objectively not effective in getting engagement either

i hope y'all can forgive the memetic hook bc the focus isn't on kegan stages LOL (who cares what i am right now, we don't know! doesn't matter! inb4 "you can't just adopt kegan 5, you have to be on a path like me blahblah" mansplainers - i kno hunni) but it IS on being able to see multiple sides, identify what's actually broken, and promote productive dialogue to fix it together

text:
I have a theory for political discourse: tpot, with all its knowledge about inner work and self-healing, can solve the political discourse issue by adapting IFS/parts work (also Aletheia, and even nondual) frameworks to conversations about politics.

Here are some rules I came up with, and I'm totally open to riffing on them:
1) Both conversational partners must ground, as if you were each going into your Self energy
1) You speak to your conversational partner with the same compassion as you would to one of your "parts" when you are in Self energy
2) BUT you must maintain low ego, such that you do not assume yourself to be more validly in Self than the other person. If you assume authority eg. "I am Self energy, and the other person is a meager part" that is egoic and creates imbalance
3) Ultimately, you adopt the view here that neither of you are Self here, but rather, Parts in a system where the Self is the system you are a part of
4) Self takes on the character of principles that are Good, Beautiful, and True that you can both agree upon
5) The objective here is discourse to achieve a harmionious, functional political system that operates on the principles of Self

Open to thoughts!

text:
Someone on Reddit translated Chinese to English in the Chinese promo video for the new Skullpanda Art Doll collection and I was pleasantly surprised at how much more poetic and layered the Chinese marketing was when compared to the US marketing. Eg. The collection is called "You found me!" in the US but "Insomnia Night Theater" in China

There's a lot of tangents here about cultural context that I won't get into in one tweet haha

I absolutely loveeee when cute things have unsettling details and dark, complex, and heartfelt lore and a lot of it can be lost in translation!

Here's the post if you're curious:
https://t.co/TpMZICncAA

It's fascinating how there's more story and high concept in other cultures' marketing but it gets watered down for US marketing. I wonder what else I don't know about bc it's not communicated by the time it reaches US consumers. A lot of joy I get from playful products IS actually from evocative, inner child symbolism and remediation which is why this collection brings me so much joy

If anyone wants to share lore you had to dig for that just made you love something more, you're welcome to :D

the dumbest example/reasoning was one person being ~6 months younger than me and me going "no, he needs to be older bc usually that's better right?" but everything else checked out (you're not rly active on twitter so i don't worry about this much but i will LOL if you're reading this)

i'm learning that if the other person and i feel deeply seen, and chemistry, connection, and compatibility come easy, i should just give it a chance

text:
has anyone else notice their enneagram change a lot? now that i'm not in social survival mode where i constantly have to meditate in real-time to manage a reactive partner that i can lash out any time, and the ppl around me are genuinely supportive and kind, and my nervous system is chilled out, i realize i'm testing away from enneagram 9 and consistently into enneagram 2. i feel more safety to express myself and there's this active 3 wing that feels excited to build and feels socially supported while i'm doing so.

i think the enneagram 9 was me in survival mode. "i must be in calm, angelic, perfectly regulated mode so that i don't set you off, so you don't swear or yell or threaten to break up with me for the 100th time, etc etc" - don't worry, all this is 1.5 years old at this point and now my standards are where they should have always been. also, when in was in the pain of heartbreak i leaned HARD into spirituality and nonduality to bypass and dissociate (finding comfort in unity with the universe in my loneliness) which further tested me into 9. since then, i've integrated and my spirituality isn't bypassy and it isn't the focus or fixation. i still believe in nondual frameworks and i do practice but it's not like it's my entire, embodied experience in the present. and i'm not saying enneagram 9s are inherently using spirituality to bypass hard feelings! there's plenty of integrated and embodied 9s. personally i think it's cool to see integration and a shift and i expect to keep shifting

text:
I didn't even realize it was Saturday instead of Sunday until 5pm today so I declined social plans and worked all day to meet Monday deadlines and then realized what happened when my friend texted "enjoy your chill Saturday" and my post on IG from the morning says "Sunday morning workshop" 🙈

Life by myself, working by myself, mostly no one else around 90% of the time - it's up to me to register the passage of time and it's mostly marked by what I accomplish or what I last did. Days of the week and the names of months and dates kind of don't register anymore. It's September already? What? It's Thursday already? What?

text:
sometimes when i describe my sexual preferences when it comes to women as a bi woman, i notice my straight, platonic female friends get a lil quiet and moody if they're clearly different from those preferences. the "i want validation from other women" engine (even nonsexual validation) goes hard, but i promise, it's better this way :')

ur friend not being attracted to you doesn't mean she doesn't see you as beautiful or that you're any less attractive! woman <> woman sexual attraction is different than woman <> man sexual attraction. bi women can still think you're extremely beautiful but there will still be 0% sexual attraction in them towards you. ask yourself: does it matter even? especially if you're straight? because that's just seeking validation! and you will, probabilistically, get sexual validation from a lot more straight males which is what you functionally desire in a partner anyway

also female <> female friendships are a LOT cleaner when there's 0% sexual attraction! we need platonic relationships too lol

text: idk how to make sense of reincarnation as a concept. my framework of reality sees everything as spontaneous emergence/dissolution in nonlinear space-time reified by localized consciousness. i have a friend and i think "he's on his last lifetime before moksha" but idk what that or even moksha means when we're all schrodinger's kittens. is it the transition from hyper-presence into omnipresence? then what happens to the element that is always you in this life? dissolution marked by the end of a series of localized consciousness into omnipresent consciousness/observer? this paradoxically seems to imply linearity and duality so i wonder why moksha isn't described as an evergreen present state of local consciousness

an open heart has a reflex to grip so much so that i have to tell it "it's okay if there's no one here yet". I might not feel held by anyone's arms but i start to notice the chill of air on my skin
Like hugs from a ghost
This isn't a vacuum but sometimes it's cold here
when the universe sighs I feel her currents running along my arms; I exhale, and breathe in again, here
It's just me,
an open heart, open air,
sound,
and silence

text:
i'm all about being yourself and playfully provoking the proverbial bear of conventions and stereotypes, but i have to admit there's such a thing as being so silly or open about your emotional experience that ppl actually start to think you're dumb or uncentered. there's even a higher risk of this if you're conventionally attractive, bubbly in demeanor, and/or have a high voice - and is it fair? not exactly, but in a patriarchal society there is this unconscious bias (even from good people!) towards feminine traits. irl it seems like the only thing preventing some ppl from dismissing me is friend vouches or if ppl experience me participating in real discussions

this is food for thought for myself bc i didn't intend to think about personal brand, but lately i feel like i'm underpotentiating when it comes to what i show on here. i want to challenge myself to orient towards a higher signal:noise ratio, coherence, and polish once again. it's how i started out and then i got comfortable and relaxed on here, so maybe time to go back to it

text:
How would you apply head-heart-gut pairings to friend groups or couples?

Eg. Do successful female friend trios only when the composite = 100% across head, heart, and gut?

Friend1: [x1  y1  z1]
Friend2: [x2  y2  z2]
Friend3: [x3  y3  z3]

Where columns = [Head  Heart  Gut]

? Success if:

[x1 + x2 + x3 = 100% Head]
[y1 + y2 + y3 = 100% Heart]
[z1 + z2 + z3 = 100% Gut]

Also does xn + yn + zn have to equal 100% in someone, or can someone be "imbalanced" in one (and over 100%) without necessarily taking up excessive space or being in a codependent group dynamic?

1) whenever you legitimately cry, do you mostly cry alone?

4) are you single? would it be different if you were/were not?
5) are YOU able to hold someone who's breaking down crying without there being consequences?

text:
Does anyone have any insights into market trends for product roles in industry and tech? @ctbeiser inspired me to think about the utility of PM vs other roles and I was curious about other ppls' takes!

Points and thoughts from @ctbeiser which I appreciated:
- His take is that PMs are a bad sign for a team. eg. Apple never had them
- Most of the planning/timeline questions really belong with an EM, most of the "what should we build" questions belong with a designer, most of the "what makes business sense" questions belong with a real business team
- Often the PM's job is effectively to solve that nobody knows what the company should make or what users want… at best this means they're a subject matter expert but it's better to just hire designers and engineers who care about the domain

My experience:
- In med device industry for large med device companies, I found my role (systems engineer) to be really redundant with the TPM
- In the current job market, all PM roles seem to be saturated with 100+ applications. I have multiple friends who studied various stuff with various career paths all trying to be PM
- As someone trying to pivot industry and functions, I initially thought I could pivot from systems engineering in medical devices to general tech via PM as an entry point, but getting noticed seemed near impossible with such a large applicant pool...and it was for roles that paid 30-50% less than what I would be making if I stuck to engineering
- I switch back to senior+ engineering roles and suddenly start hearing back pretty quickly, and the applicant pools for these engineering positions are <100 applied on L*nk*d*n for some of these roles

Curiosities:
- How did PM become such a catch-all target role for ppl? Is it still relevant? How is it evolving?
- Apple doesn't have PMs but Google, Meta, etc do. What's the value-add here beyond your Engineering Managers, Designers, and business team?

And ofc open to other thoughts from ppl too :)

text:
you learn to be so gracious when you clock the tiniest shreds of envy and jealousy instantly, and especially if it's from people close to you - so gracious that you've learned to not flinch and give people chances (within reason) to self-correct before taking any kind of action yourself

i've learned that some ppl are good ppl that just are experiencing some comparisons in relation for the first time and need time to process their emotions and adjust on their own; if i cut it off at the first few signs i'd be a lot more alone socially than i want to be. you can cut off toxic and repeat offenders but also design your life in a way where you know most people around you are good people that are just having human moments

text:
oddly appreciated being on the other side of jealousy for the first time in a while this past weekend! i walked into a south asian matchmaking event in LA and a cluster of cute guys were already orbiting a big influencer in the center of the room, and stayed orbiting her the whole event

i metabolized it the same evening:
- the rational ("we don't even want or attract the same type of men", "she’s killing it, go her", "someone shining in their own light doesn't take away mine", "here's what she did extremely well and how i will use it for inspiration")
- and the emotional ("i honor this feeling", "i remember what i love about myself")

it was so unexpected that it made me realize how "comfortable" i'd gotten. substantive jealousy had become foreign so feeling it again was clarifying

text: Once in a while if I feel completely taken for granted, I like to briefly take three steps back and watch a ship sink ever so slightly before stepping back in to lift it to buoyancy. text:
I audit my social life monthly and it sounds like:
1) Which social events did I feel the most myself at?
2) Which gatherings or hangouts did I enjoy the most?
3) Which ppl do I feel the most at home with? - What are traits they all have in common with each other? And with me?
4) Which ppl do I feel the most energized by? - What are traits they all have in common with each other? And with me?
5) Is there anyone in question #3 and #4 that should meet each other? Why? How do they complement each other?
6) How can I be in or create more environments to have more of what I like in #1-5?

text:
there's two types of social energies in circles with stable, gravitational cohesion: hearth and lightning

hearth = the ppl who ground energy. they feel like home and make others feel at home. this is warmth and safety
lightning = the ppl who add energy. they feel inspiring and inspire action and activity. this is excitement and magnetism

ppl have various hearth to lightning ratios too! some ppl can switch between both and some gravitate naturally towards one over the other

text:
As someone who took friendships and relationships so seriously and used to go to the world's end to keep some unbalanced ones alive, I had my share of heartbreaks. But now, thank gosh for all the people that left my life and for all the people who have entered it, especially in the last two years.

You hear platitudes that you think are dumb in the moment like "them leaving is just making room for those who are meant to stay" but it's actually true. When your heart's hurting you'll be like "stfu" but then when you look back and connect the dots you wouldn't have it ANY other way

text:
holiday season to holiday season, i always end up reflecting on how much my life has changed relationally. it's a bittersweet and clarifying process. if there's one lesson that's clarified over time, it's that you should choose your company based on who can consistently see and respect your soul across different seasons of life

thanksgiving to christmas to new year's has looked very different for me over the past few years. there were people who were eager to be around when my life looked shiny from the outside - good job, seemingly glamorous relationship, easy optics, etc. what was less visible then was that i was actually deeply unhappy in both

when those outer markers shifted, i watched parts of my social world quietly rearrange itself too. over time, it became obvious who was there for proximity, networking, aesthetics, or "good vibes", and who was never really relating deeply at all

and then there's people who have known me through multiple chapters and treated me with the same respect and regard throughout. or the ones who entered much later, but with a steadiness that makes it clear we’re choosing each other as friends for all the right reasons. those are the relationships that age well. the rest tend to reveal themselves eventually

overall, i feel like my discernment is sharper than ever, and my love for the people who are in my life is deeper than ever

text:
Y'alllll my friend just complimented my hair today and it made my night bc a year ago I got a "try-not-to-cry when leaving the hair salon" haircut - the person used thinning shears all the way down my hair and gave me a wolf cut. But now my natural curls and waves are almost completely back and it took a whole year to grow out 🥹

Before and after

It's still not 100% but if you have naturally wavy-curly hair do NOT ever let anyone use thinning/texturizing shears on it. I feel like salons that are used to clients with naturally straight hair or heat-styled hair don't rly understand this and will sometimes even insist on using the shears to "blend" your hair - but if you have naturally wavy-curly hair it just destroys natural volume and curl clumping patterns, and makes hair stringy at the ends

text:
a few things!
1) this happens bc your layers aren't connecting to each other, so avoid getting any more layers, or shortening the shortest layers any further
2) every ~2-4 months, get a dusting trim on the longer layers which is 0.5-1 inch off the ends to make them less stringy and make sure it's a blunt cut/point cut to blend them in
3) at 6+ months you can ask for a more substantial cut to blend all layers but still mostly keep the length
4) and neverrr let anyone use any thinning shears again. explicitly say this before the haircut and if they insist or say "just at the end", go somewhere else

text:
the digital age is so deeply dehumanizing. someone you've never met can ask to reschedule a first date and then forget about follow-up completely. someone can hear about you and maybe see a photo and be like "eh, not my type" but then try to backtrack after crossing paths in person. (both of these things have happened this year)

often it's that people are "just another person on an app" until you see them in motion in person and with context, and then *instant humanization*. before that, it's just ppl constantly ignoring each other. it's not that people are interchangeable, but without context and embodiment we are illegible

i do see this as a case for more shared-context mixers with attention to funnel and in-event interaction design

"don't you meet your type while you're out?" no, because they're more likely to be at home too, even though they're not actual homebodies

i hate that artist sets i want to see have to start at 1-2am and end at 3am. i feel forced to give up music i enjoy or else face my week's structure and sleep going out the window, and miss out on weekend morning events. this is WHY there's a high concentration of "idk what i want or how to even converse, i'm just here for a good time" peter pans at the club - the set times eliminate a large population of people who are intentional in their approach to life and prioritizing structure (not all, but many)

i love that LA is starting to have more outdoor park shows that end at 10 or 11pm because of city noise ordinances though LOL

text:
as someone that historically "likes most people by default", i fall into empathizing deeply with peoples' life stories that "made them develop these habits" and extending grace as a result, but in this era i also honor my own vision for myself and i know when to let something go and do it pretty quickly

it's insanely hard to watch sweet people become jaded, defensive, and prickly about love and dating. it's hard to watch them get withdrawn and cynical after being burnt. it's hard to watch them trade free-flowing love for a guarded ledger. the fact that i view them as fundamentally good and likable makes this hard

but i've been this way before, and sometimes after certain experiences i can feel myself begin to fall back into the slightest bit if cynicism too - but then i remember this is a big world and i do know what open-hearted love looks like. so i understand it all, and i could have been there if it wasn't for the unconditional love i see healthy couples around me demonstrate, and the open-heartedness within my closest friend circles

as someone who empathizes deeply and sees good in people, i actually have to fight graciousness with logic. i don't take it upon myself to "show someone different" anymore, and the mental frame sounds like:
1) i can't; this is theirs to work through
2) i will inevitably leave with cuts all over if i try
3) staying enables continuation of the same dynamics bc it shows them that whatever this is works for both of us, even when it fundamentally doesn't work for me and is low-key making me feel bad
4) i have my own standards that i meet myself, so why settle for anything that diverges from them so hard when this world is huge and filled with so many people

there's a part of me that goes "am i being too harsh and exacting?" but then i also remind myself that what i want absolutely matters, and the alternative is self-abandonment. if you do that, you're cooked

it's insanely hard to watch sweet people become jaded, defensive, and prickly about love and dating. it's hard to watch them get withdrawn and cynical after being burnt. it's hard to watch them trade free-flowing love for a guarded ledger. the fact that i view them as fundamentally good and likable makes this hard

but i've been this way before, and sometimes after certain experiences i can feel myself begin to fall back into the slightest bit if cynicism too - but then i remember this is a big world and i do know what open-hearted love looks like. so i understand it all, and i could have been there if it wasn't for the unconditional love i see healthy couples around me demonstrate, and the open-heartedness within my closest friend circles

as someone who empathizes deeply and sees good in people, i actually have to fight graciousness with logic. i don't take it upon myself to "show someone different" anymore, and the mental frame sounds like:
1) i can't; this is theirs to work through
2) i will inevitably leave with cuts all over if i try
3) staying enables continuation of the same dynamics bc it shows them that whatever this is works for both of us, even when it fundamentally doesn't work for me and is low-key making me feel bad
4) i have my own standards that i meet myself, so why settle for anything that diverges from them so hard when this world is huge and filled with so many people

there's a part of me that goes "am i being too harsh and exacting?" but then i also remind myself that the alternative is self-abandonment, and what i want absolutely matters

text:
re: more graciousness towards dating prospects

i'm confronting something in me right now where i realize what felt like discernment to me can be premature in dating contexts. if i make the standard for men i just met the ones set by friends' MO without any room for nuance, i miss these points allowing for more grace towards dating prospects:

1) i shouldn't over-idealize friends. friends have had MANY in-person interactions over time so we know each other well (what lights the other person up, giving grace where it's due, trusting the other person is "fundamentally good" and "didn't mean it that way" so being quick to forgive) vs. someone you go on dates with meeting you for the first time. if you cut something off with someone you met more recently, you don't get to see unfolding and convergence if you don't stay curious and amenable. what matters is how each of you take and respond to the other persons' feedback

2) nerves shouldn't be mislabled as instability. nerves during dating are real, even for generally confident people. time and time again, men are chill on the first date and then stakes hit them the second or third date bc that's when i go from "random woman i just met" to "oh, i like her". and it causes people to act differently, initially, and this DOES smooth out over time. it doesn't actually make them unstable or lacking in confidence

3) caution shouldn't be misconstrued as a lack of confidence. my friends are solidly my friends, but i think back to how much "observation" some of them who were interested at points stayed in before even hinting at any kind of move (and before platonic boundaries became clear) and many ARE confident people. it had to have been like 3-4 group hangs spread out over months, at minimum. it's actually okay to be "initially intimidating" or warrant "i need to move carefully" and it won't stay that way once people get to know you, but dating throws someone into "move gradually" and with less context

4) the scaffolding of group energy is missing in 1x1 dating. there's values, friend group cultural context, and coregulation in group dynamics. if your friend group is warm, social, accepting, and generous, everyone within softens and embodies these traits. in 1x1 dating contexts, you don't get to observe how someone is generally, and they aren't shaped by the culture around them either

so friends might show me a baseline and set many standards, but i am learning that a direct comparison is a false equivalency. ofc my nervous system feels more grounded around friends; we've all known each other longer and gotten so much time together over time. more in-person context is always going to confer more legibility

i'm still learning to soften in ways and be more receptive to seeing unfolding that might look different than what i'm used to. i do feel a lot of safety around friends but i think it's also a mistake to discount any dynamics that don't "start out" feeling like that because the familiar is always going to feel inherently safe but letting the unfamiliar unfold be potentially rewarding

text:
the mutual count and list for accounts is such a strong calibration check in some scnenarios. the bubbles i'm in on twitter are so strong bc i see something like this and think "??? what girls? who is seh talking about? do we know the same ppl?" and then i check and we only have 1 mutual despite a tweet with 3.3k likes from an 8.8k follower account, so the answer is "no". is it a faultless calibration check? not at all. but every time i'm like "??????" it does check out

AND if you are disturbed by an account's posting but you have a lot of mutuals, i think that's a sign to actually question the kinds of bubble(s) you're in - periodic questioning is healthy

text:
I used to be like "sometimes I wish I was a 6'4" man so I could just go to concerts alone and worry about nothing" but now I've figured it out:
1) be completely sober
2) dress for the female gaze. The idea is to still be cool enough to get adopted by a group of vibey women there
3) dance and stay in motion so ppl can't hold your attention; nest yourself among women or the gays and selectively give them your attention and that's how you get adopted
4) "sorry I have a bf!" and the *cut* hand gesture is the most effective and quickest way to get someone's attention off you if you don't want it, even if it's a lie

a lighter personal anecdote: i think of how i thought i was missing out on dating apps, but then i got hinge access randomly re-instated dec 31, 2025 after almost a year of no dates, and i realized i wanted it in the background to know that however/whoever i date is a choice even given all the standard methods people have at their disposal

we desire choice and choice out of abundance over coercion and scarcity

text:
i'm pondering how much of what i think i need isn't because i need the thing exactly, but rather some kind of external validation from it - or feeling like the choices i am making are out of equity and abundance over scarcity

eg. i ran a viral dating survey experiment in late march 2025 because i got kicked off hinge in feb 2025 and i was like "how am i supposed to meet people to date now" and every guy i connected with was like "wait, so what's wrong with you? why can't you find dates like normal people?" and i was like "...idk, i'm not finding my type" and i FEEL the "hot but crazy" stereotype being preemptively projected onto me constantly (so much more i can say about this - also it's.. untrue)

despite high effort, i don't even go on a single date the whole year UNTIL a friend setup in nov

dec 31, 2025 rolls around and my hinge ban randomly gets repealed, notifications go off, i start getting flooded with likes. i realize that i don't even want it right now; i just wanted "proof" that i could be asked out on dates and get matches. nervous system immediately relaxes; narratives i had in my head like "what IS wrong with you nadia, no one wants to take you out" die instantly. and the kicker is that my efforts and energy are in fact focused elsewhere now, and it's hard to care/invest in "purely random" people off the bat. but i also realized how "deeply nice" it is to have the same tools as everyone else

text:
I am experiencing a lot of rage lately and it's because men ARE treating me well, so the contrast is making me feel like I got scammed out of my entire 20s by horribly manipulative ppl. It keeps hitting me over and over. And no, the rage is not directed at anyone, but it burns to experience. I know it's low-key anger at myself for not knowing better above all

Imagine being forced to agree that the sky is green and then losing your mind over it, and getting told you're crazy for even believing that the sky is blue, and then being worn down and told you're crazy for feeling distressed over this, and then being out of that and now in a world where most ppl are like "ah yes, the sky is blue". That's what this feels like. I feel soooo much rage. So. Much. Rage.

text:
Ok ppl genuinely hate going to other places for steps apparently and think it's harder than it is - takes like 2 min to switch from ChatGPT to Claude. Here it is written out

> Go to https://t.co/YaJ1uRZhHJ
> Settings
> Capabilities
> Import memory from other AI providers, click "Start import"
> Copy paste that prompt that pops up on the page into chatGPT
> Take what chatGPT gives you and paste it into the box on that same import memory pop-up on Claude

Done! Feel free to screenshot/share

text:
i feel the most social peace + connection (and i'm sure others do too) when i don't feel observed as a collection of peoples' projections and evaluations 

projections = "that's my future wife/gf", "she thinks x y z about me", "i want her to do x y z", "i want her to see me as x y z", "she's just a silly innocent girl", *insert some preconception here*

evaluations = "is she better/smarter/prettier/more interesting/dumber than me?", "is she doing anything useful to me?", "is she into me?", "should i hit on her?", "would she be into me?", *insert a bunch of noisy calculations*

you can hear these things even when ppl don't say it!! the world is a very noisy place. subtract all the above and you just get open, gentle observation at its most neutral and grounded curiosity at the most leading edge of connection

Re: Discernment in tantra in the face of charismatic ppl (eg. teachers and gurus) embodying everything that could go wrong in tantra (and i mean classical tantra, not even neo-tantra) 

This is wisdom from an anon in another forum (i'm not trying to dox ppl and these aren't even my words, but i think this should be shared)

--

THE ONE RULE: Never drop awareness (viveka) in the name of freedom.

When you get into things like:
Ucchiṣṭa
Cāṇḍālinī
Piśācinī
Ḍākinī-type energies

you are dealing with forces that dissolve boundaries.

That means:
- moral boundaries
- psychological boundaries
- identity boundaries

The Trap & most common mistake: “Since everything is Śakti, I can follow any impulse.”

That’s not Tantra. That’s delusion (moha).

The Correction: Yes — everything is Śiva. But recognition (awareness, viveka) must be present. Without that, you fall into āṇava mala (limitation, confusion, contraction).

A Simple Test: Before following any impulse, ask
"Is this increasing awareness?"
or
"Is this reducing me into unconsciousness?"

If it leads to:
-clarity
- presence
- expansion
👉 That’s Śakti moving in a liberating direction.

If it leads to:
- compulsion
- confusion
- loss of control
- ego inflation
👉 That’s bondage disguised as Tantra.

The Subtle Danger: Ucchiṣṭa principle says “Nothing is impure.”
The ego twists it into: “Everything I do is fine.”
That right there is the trap.

Ground Rule: If you skip clarity, transgression becomes self-destruction.

Stay anchored in: awareness of awareness

Not in:
- emotion
- impulse
- fascination

As emphasized in texts like Vijñāna Bhairava: Remain in witnessing, even in intense states.

Key Takeaways:
Freedom without awareness = bondage
Transgression without clarity = downfall

Go as deep (into embodiment, shakti downward triangle descent) as you want — but never lose the witness.

--

text:
would you believe that falling in love isn't even the hard part? people are so beautiful. i genuinely like people. there's an abundance of qualities i admire in literally everyone i'm close to. data shows me that i am pretty easy to like and love too. the love exists. the hard part is "i trust you equally or more than i trust myself to do a good job at life" and "life with you works better and more easily than me taking care of all of it myself"

i know that sounds clinical and evaluative, but if you are giving and love people by default you have to be discerning - or the world will drain you. people may want and want from you (energy, time, validation) without ever asking about what you need. it's easier to have your boundaries around this with friends that you still love deeply, but it's crucial to choose carefully when it's a forever-roommate and father of your kids. the wrong person that takes more than they give is a liability, and the right person that makes your life easier than if you were simply alone is an asset. love is abundant and easy; i love easily and often. you can love a lot of people, but it's the act of choosing just one and choosing wisely that's the important one.

text:
hair products + tips post for ppl with my hair type which is a rly finnicky one. for a while, my frizzy, curly hair was a big insecurity but now i like it bc i know how to style it - it took a lot of trial and error to figure out. i have natural 2B/3A hair that frizzes and changes personality a lot based on environmental conditions or how it dries

how do you know if this is your hair?:
- if you put nothing in your hair, longer layers are looser waves while shorter layers curl or frizz a lot
- if it rains, your hair gets frizzier or curlier
- if you go by the beach, your hair forms ringlets
- a LOT of south asian women (but def not all) have this hair type and i see many straighten it instead of embracing it! do whatever you like ofc, but you can also try this

i found i prefer my hair texture with natural 2B wavy bc of the lasting power during the week with hair products that i can't even feel - i CAN make it 2C or 3A if i want to on wash day with curl creams and the ringlets look cute, but the next day my hair overall feels stickier with the curl cream in it so i don't do this often. also i don't use heat tools!

products/routine:
- shampoo hair only once or twice a week so you don't dry out your scalp + natural oils (but also i only rly gym like ~3 times a week; you might want to do it more if you go more)
- shampoo is pureology strength cure bc i do have color-treated hair. BUT also i recommend not over-bleaching hair if you care about natural texture; you will destroy your waves. get subtle top-layer balayage + scattered inner highlights only and lift only 2 levels max
- conditioner is the costco kirkland hydrate dupe one
- color maintenance is keracolor purple mixed with mocha on top of blonde-brown highlights as needed on fading
- after wash: microfiber towel to squeeze out excess water
- on damp hair: color wow curly hair coat on every 4th wash only (more and your hair will be greasy). use your fingers or a wide-tooth comb to disperse product. no brushes or fine-tooth combs
- not your mother's curl talk leave-in conditioner on damp hair. again, use your fingers or a wide-tooth comb to disperse product. if it feels sticky on drying - you are using too much. if it's still frizzing, you are using too little
- no heat-styling, air-dry only. DON'T TOUCH IT while it's drying. don't even lie down. this creates frizz
- silk pillowcase to sleep and reduce frizz

text:
re: islamophobia

i grew up muslim until age ~13 where i went through a phase of atheism before meditatively finding nondualism at ~16-17 (which makes the most sense to me now from an epistemic and ontological standpoint), but i don't believe in islamophobia. in the 9/11 era i was 6 years old and grew up surrounded by it; i felt ashamed to even exist and it deeply impacted my self-confidence into the 2010s

ppls' broad, deeply negative perceptions of islam form when they don't separate the concepts and mechanisms of dogma, culture, political strategy, and personal practice from each other. i think these distinctions are important. if you don't actually know many muslim communities from the inside and across different countries, and you have deeply negative biases, you're probably painting entire populations with a broad brush dipped in media propaganda or in stories from traumatized muslim ppl who left the religion - instead of sourcing understanding from ppl who do practice it and appreciate it in the modern day.

yes, i have my critiques with dogma in general (again, nondualist over here) but i don't think this is unique to islam as a religion or even to religion itself (political lables, rigid alignment too!). islamophobia, however, is usually an uninformed and uncontextualized phobia

text:
i have a lot more social commentary now that so many of my friends are getting coupled off and i am coupled off (disclaimer: this is assuming everyone is monogamous/closed relationship perspective. disregard if you're poly - idek how to talk about poly bc i am not poly) and one of them is jealousy within relationships over other ppl

this is inspired by a trend where male friends' new gfs get visibly insecure when they meet me. i immediately try to be as warm and acknowledging of their existence as i am with my friend's existence. they relax a little when they learn i have a bf but they're still on edge bc they're new (i understand this, trust me, i do) and i think this is making my life easier than when it was single (bc it hurts my heart to have to go cold with friends! but i'd do it if their relationship needed it) but it would be cool if it could be easy regardless. i would want them to know that these are *genuinely* good men. it's insanity to think that you, or me, or any other person in this world is the *only* attractive person in a world of billions - this is just not true. yes, ppl register other ppl as attractive here and there, but the act of *loving and choosing* the one you're with is what good men do, and they do it quite easily.

there's a stark energetic difference in taken, loyal men and single ones or those who are inclined to disrespect their partner in any capacity - the difference is night and day. even before men say they're taken i can tell they're taken and loyal; the posture is just *different*. their energy lands like "that's a wife guy/happy in love dude that just happens to like people". they have and respect boundaries. noticing someone's appearance is natural; fixating on them to the point where your partner notices and feels neglected is not. warmth and general caring towards people is natural; flirtation and courtship outside your relationship is not. some deeply caring and high capacity men are actually able to be caring to their partner AND their female friends and this is healthy too (eg. helping gf + her friend move heavy things, this is pretty damn normal friend behavior) as long as it doesn't take from primary attention to his partner, and it lands as clearly platonic bc this is just a good, caring person. if he's attentive, loyal, and treats you with complete respect and priority then you're GOOD. 

i don't even run into the wandering-eye type; the friends i keep are good dudes. for these men, arbitrary women or his female friends, no matter how beautiful they are, do not mean what you do to your partner. it's not even close - there's a lot more to loyalty and love than absolute external beauty or even a good personality - there's your entire story and all of you. if you adopt a dog and you love this dog, you might notice other cute dogs playing in the dog park but most ppl don't just go "hm, i want that dog instead of my dog" even though that dog might also be a wonderful dog (and i'm not comparing people to pets! this is to illustrate nonfungibility through memories and shared experiences, and love for a specific entity - dog, cat, sibling, you)

if the person you're dating is a good person, you can mostly trust their judgement in friends and their ability to cut off people if/when someone even attempts to cross a boundary. if they don't cut the boundary-pusher off, cut THEM off. but if all these people are good people, things like this just don't happen.

text:
If you're a woman in your 30s who wants kids I recommend you clearly communicate your timeline for when you want to be having kids if you're dating a man in his 20s, and make sure you're aligned. There's a good chance thinking about lifestyle and finances for a home + kids hasn't hit him with the same intensity as you or men in their 30s

If you're a guy in your 20s dating a woman in her 30s there's a decent chance you will encounter a mindset and prioritization shift when it comes to family and financial planning. You can rise to it, but you might feel the push earlier than it otherwise would have happened if you're dating someone the same age or younger

I haven't dated ppl who are substantially younger (like 4+ years) bc intuition just tells me they'll feel the difference in life stages and age like they're in a pressure cooker. But I know of couples where the guy is a lot younger and it's worked! But I do recommend communicating these things early so there's no surprises and you guys are on the same page :)

text: complementary perspective: as someone who had a bf at the start of college and a couple of years after (6 years!) and didn't ever date before then, i think this is also due to just being so hyperintentional and fixated on a commitment that you don't get to experience different men until later in life to get data on what "good" and "bad" men look like (or experience "good" men at all) until much, much later. bc of a long commitment to someone not for me, it's all i knew for a while. no reference point for "good" means dating the same type over and over and repeating patterns (que repetitive compulsion + another 2-3 years dating someone like him - then BOOM, you're single again while your friends who dated more with a lot more reference points for what's good vs. bad are having babies)

text: i notice that events with only friends + friends of friends tend to bank on a friend group culture of thoughtfulness, generosity, and attunement. in these environments, no one questions how their friends will show up; these stay largely not-for-profit. they only start to become for-profit when it's a bunch of friends collaborating and opening the experience to the public, where financial compensation becomes what's exchanged for giving energy that might be variably reciprocated/not reciprocated at all

text:
biotech and medical devices as industries sorely needs modernization, and i want to collect more thoughts on this (feel free to share yours) but here are some of mine:

1) RECRUITING/HIRING. if you're a recruiter in biotech or medical devices: i want you to focus on modernizing this industry to not miss out on hiring talent between the ages of 23-35!! eg. i am noticing that many of the biotech and medical device hubs in southern california are FAR from LA. you need to have remote/flexible options bc young ppl (for your L4-L6 open requisitions you're looking at ages ~26-32 given it's 4-8 years experience) don't wanna move to places were the average population age is filled with middle-aged homeowners and their teenage children when what THEY want outside worklife is to meet ppl their age, date, and be in a lively city. there's great opportunities and cool tech that i won't even consider bc it's 1-2 hours driving ONE WAY away from LA even though it's otherwise a great fit bc i know i would be miserable with that commute or move and give up so much

2) DIGITAL STRATEGY x AI. your systems need parallel development with not only your devices but also digital and cloud-hosted AI strategy, and AI tooling to get work done internally. this is the AI age. software moves fast. you need to stay up to date with AI for data processing and analytics and develop in parallel with hardware and software so you can scale and update infrastructure. internal tooling needs to stay up to date with big tech trends to make work fast and easy. yes, you still need regulatory body audit trails (i understand this is what makes adoption slower) but you can and should develop and design for this. you need focused roles on this

i welcome any more insights and thoughts! i had an interview with an open-minded recruiter and hiring team recently on some of this stuff and i welcome giving feedback to any more ppl involved in recruiting, hiring, and team/resource strategy!
